title: What is Multiplication?
intro: Multiplication is a fast way to add the same number again and again. Instead of adding, we can multiply!

keyPoints.heading: Equal Groups
keyPoints.text: 3 groups of 4 stars is the same as 4 + 4 + 4 = 12. We write this as 3 × 4 = 12.

keyPoints.heading: Arrays
keyPoints.text: An array is dots arranged in rows and columns. 3 rows of 4 dots = 12 dots, so 3 × 4 = 12.

worked_example.problem: A farmer has 4 baskets. Each basket has 5 apples. How many apples are there in total?
steps: There are 4 equal groups, and each group has 5 apples.

title: Multiplication Facts for 3, 4, 5 and 10
intro: Knowing your multiplication facts by heart helps you solve problems quickly, without counting one by one.

keyPoints.heading: ×5 pattern
keyPoints.text: The answer always ends in 0 or 5. It is half of the ×10 fact: 6 × 5 is half of 6 × 10 = 60, so 6 × 5 = 30.

keyPoints.heading: ×4 pattern
keyPoints.text: Double, then double again! 4 × 6: double 6 is 12, double 12 is 24. So 4 × 6 = 24.

keyPoints.heading: ×3 pattern
keyPoints.text: Double the number, then add one more group. 3 × 7: double 7 is 14, plus 7 more is 21.

worked_example.problem: Use the doubling pattern to work out 4 × 8.

title: What is Division?
intro: Division means splitting a number into equal groups. It is the opposite of multiplication.

keyPoints.heading: Sharing division
keyPoints.text: 12 lollies shared equally between 3 friends. Each friend gets 4 lollies. 12 ÷ 3 = 4.

keyPoints.heading: Grouping division
keyPoints.text: 12 lollies put into groups of 4. That makes 3 groups. 12 ÷ 4 = 3.

worked_example.problem: There are 15 cupcakes. They are put into boxes of 5. How many boxes are needed?

title: Multiplication and Division are Related
intro: Multiplication and division are inverse (opposite) operations. Knowing one helps you check the other!

keyPoints.heading: Fact families
keyPoints.text: 3 × 4 = 12, 4 × 3 = 12, 12 ÷ 3 = 4, 12 ÷ 4 = 3 all belong to the same fact family.

keyPoints.heading: Check your work
keyPoints.text: If you divide and get an answer, multiply it back to check! 20 ÷ 4 = 5, and 4 × 5 = 20 checks out.

keyPoints.heading: Two-step problems
keyPoints.text: Some real problems need more than one step, mixing multiplication or division with addition or subtraction.

worked_example.problem: Sam buys 3 packs of pencils. Each pack has 6 pencils. He gives away 5 pencils. How many pencils does he have left?
